functions: Forkhead box transcription factor that shapes chromatin accessibility at senescence-specific enhancers
Interacts with p300/CBP to stimulate H3K27 acetylation and open chromatin at pro-inflammatory SASP gene enhancers
Coordinates the pro-inflammatory senescence-associated secretory phenotype (SASP) program
Mesenchymal transcription factor driving epithelial-mesenchymal transition (EMT) signatures
Promotes myeloid differentiation while suppressing T-cell differentiation in hematopoietic progenitors (lineage specification factor)
Activating forkhead factor that binds herpesviral genome nonsequence-specifically and acetylates/opens viral chromatin with CBP/P300 to promote lytic replication
Transcriptional activator of EZH2 mediating epigenetic silencing of DKK3, controlling tubular cell senescence and renal fibrosis
Lung endothelial-specific transcription factor that binds the S1PR1 (S1pr1) promoter to drive its expression
pathways: Transcriptional regulation by FOX (forkhead) transcription factors
PRC2 methylates histones and DNA (EZH2/H3K27me3-mediated silencing)

disease_associations.disease: B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (FOXF1/FENDRR subtype, IGH::FENDRR rearrangement)
T-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia / mixed phenotype acute leukemia (t(14;16)(q32;q24), BCL11B enhancer hijacking)

pubmed.druggability_notes: FOXF1/2-mediated enhancer remodeling is proposed as a key target for modulating senescent cells to promote healthy aging; FOXF1 acts via its epigenetic cofactor interactions (p300/CBP), which represent potential intervention points. In leukemia subtypes overexpressing FOXF1, subtype-specific targeted interventions and early immunotherapy (e.g., blinatumomab) are suggested. The FOXF1-EZH2-DKK3 axis and iron/ROS-FOXF1 feedback loop are proposed as therapeutic targets in renal and pulmonary fibrosis. No direct small-molecule binding pocket described.

functions: Probable transcription activator for lung-specific genes
Sequence-specific DNA binding at RNA Pol II cis-regulatory regions
Positive regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II
Regulation of lung development and lung vasculature development
Regulation of cardiac and blood vessel development (e.g., cardiac left ventricle morphogenesis, ductus arteriosus closure)
Regulation of digestive tract, pancreas, and ureter development
Component of transcription regulator complex / chromatin binding
cellular_states: Cardiac tissue (heart/left ventricle morphogenesis, endocardial cushion)

disease_associations.disease: Disease variant (unspecified developmental disorder associated with FOXF1 variants)

uniprot.druggability_notes: No druggability information, binding pockets, or drug classes mentioned in the provided text. As a nuclear DNA-binding transcription factor, it is generally considered a challenging therapeutic target.

functions: Probable transcription activator for a number of lung-specific genes
Transcription factor involved in lateral plate mesoderm formation

disease_associations.disease: Alveolar capillary dysplasia with misalignment of pulmonary veins
